content:
Let $n=[r]$ and $f=\{r\}$, so $r=n+f$ with $n\in\{0,1,2,\dots\}$ and $0\le f<1$.  The equation 
$$25f+n=125$$ 
gives 
$$f=\frac{125-n}{25},$$ 
and we need $0\le f<1$.  Thus 
$$0\le\frac{125-n}{25}<1\;\Longrightarrow\;0\le125-n<25\;\Longrightarrow\;100<n\le125.$$ 
Since $n$ is an integer, $n=101,102,\dots,125$.  Then
$$r=n+f=n+\frac{125-n}{25}=\frac{24n+125}{25}.$$
Hence the sum of all such $r$ is
\[
\sum_{n=101}^{125}\frac{24n+125}{25}
=\frac{1}{25}\Bigl(24\sum_{n=101}^{125}n+125\cdot25\Bigr)
=\frac{1}{25}\bigl(24\cdot2825+3125\bigr)
=\frac{70925}{25}
=2837.
\]
Therefore the desired sum is \(\boxed{2837}.\)

message.content:
